title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Command Workshop
summary: Resolve a multi-layer workstation incident and hand off a safe, reproducible operating procedure to another technician. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.
Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.
Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 6.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Operations Handoff condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the workshop evidence note for Operations Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 5.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
